---
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# When people come

Which days of the week draw the most attendance? Counts below are registrations (a proxy for
headcount), grouped by the event's performance date.

```{python}
#| label: setup-time
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from hh.io import read_parquet
from hh.viz import style, CATEGORICAL

regs = read_parquet("processed", "registrations_enriched.parquet")

# attendance by weekday for the performance sub-types + classes
focus = regs[regs["event_minorcat"].isin(["theater", "music", "dance", "opera"])].copy()
focus["facet"] = "performance"
clz = regs[regs["event_majorcat"] == "class"].assign(facet="class")
combo = pd.concat([focus.assign(cat=focus["event_minorcat"]), clz.assign(cat="class")])
combo = combo.dropna(subset=["weekday"])
tab = combo.groupby(["weekday", "cat"], observed=True).size().reset_index(name="attendance")
tab
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-weekday-by-cat
#| fig-cap: "Attendance by day of week, by category"
order = ["theater", "music", "dance", "opera", "class"]
fig = px.bar(
    tab, x="weekday", y="attendance", color="cat",
    category_orders={"cat": order}, barmode="group",
    color_discrete_sequence=CATEGORICAL,
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Day of week", yaxis_title="Registrations (attendance proxy)", legend_title=""
)
style(fig).show()
```

**Theater peaks Saturday; music peaks Sunday.** Classes spread across the week with a Friday peak.
To answer "how far do people come for theater on Friday/Saturday vs Sunday," slice the same table by
distance band:

```{python}
#| label: tab-theater-day-band
theater = regs[regs["event_minorcat"] == "theater"].dropna(subset=["weekday", "distance_band"])
(theater.groupby(["weekday", "distance_band"], observed=True).size()
        .unstack("distance_band").fillna(0).astype(int))
```
